author: @gregorybrazell5133
text:
I don't think there will be a 9th episode... but...

THIS is evidence of the problem with Hollywood, and with cinema as a whole.  Fans only have so many years, so much time.  The fans are HOPING there is a 9th episode because otherwise it means they wasted TEN YEARS of their lives.  Sure, not an actual 10 years... but, they followed, they discussed, they focused on, a series for 10 years.

IF the finale is as horrible as the one we received?  It means the Duffers robbed all of us of a full decade of our lives.  This is time we can't replace.  It isn't just a trick or troll... it is an absolute loss of our lives.  It is like having a spouse who is found to be cheating... we cannot get back those years.

So, if they do not have something like this planned... then they should never get another day of work, ever.  They should pay back all bits of money they gained during the production.

author: @zeroryuj6093
text:
Watched the EFAP crew discuss how this series works from it's inception to modern day...They HAD a plan for season 1 and nothing after as it was never intended to BE a series, it was supposed to be a one off, now they write it as they go, there is no plan, they never thought they'd have to explain so many things (that's why it doesn't make sense anymore) a LOT of the better characters arc choices and plot points...came from the actors not the Duffer brothers. There are plot holes, inconsistencies and retcons throughout the series. the fans crafting all these theories have put more thought into the show than the people making it ever did. Fans are now COUNTING on things the Duffer brothers NEVER even thought of. They have severe issues even keeping things coherent from season to season and fans think they have this hyper intricate plan...they don't.

This is the "the plan was always for the emperor to come back" situation all over again.

author: @daywither927
text: Putting aside the general stupidity, I do not think the Duffers have neither the brains nor the balls to pull anything of the sort. To be clear, there have been weird-out endings to series in the past, even with mainstream ones. The Prisoner, a late 60s british series and one of the all time greats: the final episode of that is a fully experimental psychedelic twist that reframes the whole thing. But that is a wholly different league from Stranger Things.

author: @TommyCampari
text:
If halfway through the epilogue graduation, something crazy happened like reality breaking down, and we learned it wasn’t over, then this would honestly be one of the best television episodes ever made.

The ending we got was so in-inspiring and unsatisfying. No questions answered. No villain motivations revealed. All we got was a big CGI monster killed in a CGI world with water balloons and an m16.

Seeing these fake promos are getting me so hyped. What a complete wasted opportunity.

author: @sliksick123
text: At this point, I am genuinely convinced that Conformity Gate is an extremely subtle passive-aggressive collaboration by everyone on Stranger Things but the two writers as the most epic but subtle middle finger to both show how bad the writing for the final season was and how dedicated the fans are, as well as revealing what could have been have Netflix had not forced their hand. Like legitimately, it would be poetic irony to show that everyone but the two writers knew the show better than them, and the writers were huffing so much copium they are now probably convinced their subconscious minds were rebelling against them, hence the inconsistencies.

author: @DJSkandalous
text:
Not sold on it being real but if they were smart, they would have filmed something so it doesnt go down like Lost and GOT. If people love episode 8, you just keep it vaulted or drop it in 10 years as a lost episode. If there's backlash, you tell people on social media about a secret episode and give them clues so they run with it. Then you either have pre-made conspiracy videos sent to these social media creators to post to get people talking before the drop.

Are they this smart? Idk...but if this is what they did..it would be the single most act of free promotion ever done by a company/studio lol

author: @foxcelot2286
text:
In season 1 episode 7 at 25:03 there's a scene with the Mr. Clark there's a scene with him watching a movie with a girl which parallels the ending vecnas head coming off. Except in the movie Clark was watching they burned the villain... He says that it all just "Melted Plastic and Bubblegum..." maybe im looking into this too much but maybe their saying the death was fake... maybe further answers might lie somewhere in this episode? 

Ok future edit here season 1 episode 7 at 8:23 when the Hawkins lab comes to Mike's house as soon as they open the door theirs something like a chime followed by a ticking metronom or possibly a ticking clock as they investigate his house looking for 11. Maybe it indicates that vecna was there in that scene. The first possible chime i believe begins with Martin Brenner (papa) turns around to look at mikes dad...  a bit odd.. Maybe Papa is being controlled by vecna? Or maybe Vecna is present with the investigation team?

Ok im back again. So in an interview with actor Jamie Campbell aka Henry Creel aka vecna he mentioned there was something we missed in volume 1 of stranger things and then goes on to mention the importance of color popping in scenes saying something about "...the red tie is definitely something." I think he was hinting at season 1 episode 7 8:23 because as soon as they open the door and the "chime" happens and "clock" begins to tick There is a man at the door with the investigation team that is wearing a red tie.... this might be looking way to deep into it but it could be vecna.

author: @sehalos
text: I will take back everything that I have said about the Doofus Brothers if they deliver us with episode 9

author: @sirazazeloflowkey6424
text:
They had a great idea for one season. The first season. It was gold. It was gold and it actually made sense. You could even believe (not really, of course) that it really happened and got silenced and some such.

Starting season 2, this already became pretty impossible. Ending of season 4, we are definately in an alternative universe with it's own history. Because there is no way the people in Hawkins by this point don't know something outlandish is going on. Word would spread like wildfire, theories, tv segments etc. etc.

They should have sat on it for longer, map out a few seasons at least.

But someone said it right :
Season 2 was made without Season 3 in mind, Season 3 was made without Season 4 in mind and so on...

Also they did the classical writing mistake of redconning a bunch of things right before the ending, just to produce holes they can work on in spinoff projects. Such a dumb choice.

author: @shinyreshades4653
text:
So yeah, I didn’t believe it because it was honestly pretty stupid, but I still checked Netflix today to see if there was anything there :D

Obviously, there is no secret episode. And all those HUNDREDS of “errors” weren’t some parallel universe thing — the Duffer Brothers just don’t give a shit, they just want your money, as always.

Now it’s becoming clear: all those inconsistent mistakes that people collected as “clues”… they weren’t breadcrumbs at all — the writing was just unbelievably bad xD

That fan who came up with this idea basically buried the original Season 5.

Before, everyone was like: “Hmm, yeah, Season 5… kinda meh, but okay… fine ending.”

Now everyone’s like: “YO, EVERYTHING IS WRONG! …AND THERE ISN’T EVEN A SECRET EPISODE!” 😂

author: @603me.
text:
The theory is intriguing. I'm not betting on it. I think a lot of the alleged clues are borderline at best. But there is one, that if it is a mistake, it's a glaring omission.

At the start of the end credits for the finales of seasons 1-4, the red outline title card appears.

No title card appears during the end credits of episode 8 at any point.

author: @amjnyc79
text:
So. Let’s recap:
Season 1: ✅ 
Season 2-3: Meh
Season 4: ✅ ✅ 
Season 5: Kathleen Kennedy with a touch of David Benioff and DB Weiss.

The end.
Sound accurate?
Yup.

replies.author: @Kspice9000
replies.text:
Swap the check marks on 4 and move them to 2
 Then you might be on to something.
Season 4 was just a rancid dogshit as season 5.

replies.author: @amjnyc79
replies.text:
@K@Kspice9000ecna carried 4 whether he was on or off the screen. That was a +. They did a good job with juggling the multiple story arcs. It felt closer to season 1. It wasn’t as heavy on “the community” messaging. 
Season 4 definitely steals the checks from 5. S5 was a 180 degree turn from 4. I’d rather rewatch 4 than 5.

Season 2 was more than meh, I guess. 
It was a lot of story building, which was pretty good. I just got impatient with the slower episodes. That’s just me tho lol.

I’m not f’ing with season 5 outside of watching clips of very select scenes. Once was good enough for me. It sets my teeth on edge watching the entirety of it. It had some dope scenes, but not enough to stand out among the other bad aspects.

I’m pretty sure Netflix had heavy creative input with season 5. Season 5 falls in line with their… proclivities.
